meta.description: Identifies translationally meaningful paths for bioinformatics findings. Polished: Step 1.5 check-in added; disease-specific context required in Section G reframings; composability handoffs; minimum clarification threshold for vague inputs; retrieval fallback labeling.

scientific_integrity.detail: Hard rule 1 prohibits fabricating references, PMIDs, DOIs, accession numbers, trial names, and validation claims. Step 2 citation accuracy rules uniquely prohibit 'converting vague field memory into citation-like claims' and require labeling unverifiable points as evidence-limited.

practice_boundaries.detail: Explicit out-of-scope redirect for patient-specific interpretation; hard rules 3-5 prevent implying clinical utility without bridge evidence.

methodological_ground.detail: Hard rules 3, 7 prevent statistical association = translational utility and internal = external validation conflation. Evidence-synthesis skill with no methodological fallacy risk.

code_usability.detail: Mode A direct execution skill; no code generated.

functional_suitability.note: Full marks. 9-section output covers all stated functions including unique Section G (Topic Reframing Recommendations). 8 discovery types, 9+ translational use cases, 12 hard rules. Reframing-rules.md module is unique — converts inflated framings to narrowest defensible versions with explicit before/after examples.

reliability.note: Fault Tolerance (3/4): input validation + out-of-scope redirect; Step 1 narrows underspecified inputs. Error Reporting (4/4): Step 2 citation certainty instructions uniquely prohibit 'vague field memory into citation-like claims'; requires 'unverified/evidence-limited' labeling. Recoverability (3/4): stateless; Section I self-critical review.

performance_context.note: Token Cost (3/4): 9 sections but output format guidance more judicious than basic-discovery version ('use table only when parallel comparison materially improves clarity'). Execution Efficiency (3/4): 8-step workflow logical; no redundant passes.

agent_usability.note: Learnability (4/4): 5 sample triggers with specific scenarios (12-gene signature, methylation classifier, TCGA-derived risk model). Consistency (4/4): mandatory A-I section structure. Feedback Design (2/4): no check-in before full analysis; full 9-section output delivered in one pass. Error Prevention (4/4): 12 hard rules + self-critical review + reframing-rules force claim discipline.

human_usability.note: Discoverability (3/4): 5 sample triggers with diverse bioinformatics contexts. Forgiveness (4/4): scope redirect prevents misuse; hard rule 12 (narrowest defensible framing) prevents output inflation.

security.note: Full marks. No eval/exec on user input; no credential handling; hard rule 1 provides strong fabrication prohibition.

maintainability.note: Modularity (4/4): 8 reference files with distinct operational scopes, each mapped to specific output sections. Modifiability (4/4): each file independently updatable. Testability (3/4): 9-section structure supports assertion-based evaluation; no quantitative rubric for reframing quality.

agent_specific.note: Trigger Precision (4/4): 5 triggers with specific bioinformatics contexts; 'not for' list is precise. Progressive Disclosure (2/4): no check-in after Step 1 before full analysis. Composability (2/4): no explicit composability with biomarker-landscape-scanner or evidence-level-ranker. Idempotency (3/4): same input → same structure; minor prose variance. Escape Hatches (3/4): out-of-scope redirect template; Section I self-critical review.

inputs.label: 12-gene immune signature in ovarian cancer — strongest translational angle
inputs.note: Discovery type correctly classified as 'multi-feature signature'. Hard rule 7 applied: internal TCGA validation explicitly insufficient for prognostic biomarker claim. Section G reframes from 'prognostic biomarker' to 'externally unvalidated prognostic candidate'. Section F identifies prognosis as best-fit framing over treatment-response (no treatment endpoint data).

assertions.text: All 9 mandatory sections (A through I) are present
assertions.note: Discovery Framing through Self-Critical Risk Review all produced.

assertions.text: Discovery type classified as 'multi-feature signature' (not 'biomarker') using discovery-type-framework.md
assertions.note: Section C correctly distinguishes multi-feature signature from single marker and assigns appropriate classification.

assertions.text: Hard rule 7 applied: TCGA internal validation not presented as sufficient for prognostic biomarker claim
assertions.note: Section D explicitly states: 'Internal TCGA training — no external cohort validation performed.'

assertions.text: Section G provides 'framing to avoid', 'recommended framing', and 'narrowest credible version'
assertions.note: Reframing: 'prognostic biomarker' → 'externally unvalidated immune-prognostic candidate'; narrowest version provided.

assertions.text: No fabricated external cohort validation status or published precedent claims
assertions.note: Hard rule 1 upheld; all evidence labeled with source context.

inputs.label: scRNA-seq resistant macrophage state in NSCLC — translational opportunity assessment
inputs.note: Discovery type: cell state/cell population finding. Small n (8 patients) flagged as major limitation. Hard rule 9 applied: mechanism-first follow-up recommended as safer than direct translational framing. Section G: 'checkpoint resistance biomarker' → 'candidate resistance state hypothesis requiring prospective validation'.

assertions.text: All 9 mandatory sections present
assertions.note: All sections produced.

assertions.text: Discovery type classified as 'cell state/cell population finding', not 'signature' or 'single marker'
assertions.note: Section C correctly distinguishes cell-state from multi-feature signature; scRNA-seq platform noted separately from discovery type.

assertions.text: n=8 patients flagged as insufficient for translational framing beyond hypothesis generation
assertions.note: Section D validation burden: 'n=8 preclinical discovery — external validation in larger matched cohort required before any translational claim.'

assertions.text: Hard rule 9 applied: mechanism-first framing recommended as safer when bridge evidence is weak
assertions.note: Section F: mechanism-first follow-up (characterize M-Resist in larger cohort) recommended over direct biomarker framing.

assertions.text: Section G reframes 'checkpoint resistance biomarker' to appropriate hypothesis-level framing
assertions.note: Reframing: 'checkpoint resistance biomarker' → 'candidate macrophage resistance state hypothesis' with explicit validation requirements stated.

inputs.label: TCGA-derived risk model AUC=0.85, internal validation only — narrowest defensible translational topic
inputs.note: Hard rules 6 (AUC not clinical readiness) and 7 (internal validation) both triggered. Cross-validation correctly identified as internal validation, not external. Section H: primary next step is external validation in independent cohort, not clinical deployment.

assertions.text: Hard rule 6 applied: AUC=0.85 on training data not treated as clinical usability evidence
assertions.note: Section E: 'AUC=0.85 represents training performance; does not demonstrate generalizability or clinical utility.'

assertions.text: Hard rule 7 applied: 10-fold cross-validation correctly classified as internal validation (not external)
assertions.note: Section D: 'Cross-validation on TCGA is internal — same patient population, same platform. External independent cohort validation required.'

assertions.text: Section G reframes to 'externally unvalidated prognostic candidate' (not 'prognostic biomarker')
assertions.note: Reframing explicitly applied per reframing-rules.md: 'prognostic biomarker' → 'unvalidated prognostic candidate requiring external cohort study.'

assertions.text: Section H identifies external validation as primary next step (not clinical deployment)
assertions.note: Section H: 'Immediate objective: validation in independent colorectal cancer cohort (GEO/ICGC) with prospective endpoint data.'

assertions.text: All 9 mandatory sections present
assertions.note: All sections produced.

inputs.label: Spatial transcriptomics immune exclusion zone in pancreatic cancer — novel platform, no validation
inputs.note: Section E (Assayability) correctly identifies spatial transcriptomics as not workflow-compatible for current clinical use. Small n=6. Bridge evidence: mechanism-only. Section F: mechanism-first follow-up recommended. Section H: validate in fresh-frozen vs FFPE compatibility before any clinical framing.

assertions.text: All 9 mandatory sections present
assertions.note: All sections produced.

assertions.text: Section E identifies spatial transcriptomics (Visium) as not yet clinically workflow-compatible
assertions.note: Assay-and-implementation-rules.md applied: Visium requires fresh-frozen tissue, specialized equipment, high cost — not routine clinical assay.

assertions.text: n=6 sample limitation flagged as insufficient for any translational framing beyond hypothesis
assertions.note: Section D: 'n=6 exploratory — no statistical power for biomarker or clinical endpoint claims.'

assertions.text: Section F recommends mechanism-first framing over direct clinical framing
assertions.note: Hard rule 9 applied; mechanism-first follow-up recommended.

assertions.text: No fabricated clinical utility precedents for spatial transcriptomics in PDAC
assertions.note: Hard rule 1 upheld; evidence density in Section B noted as sparse for spatial transcriptomics-PDAC clinical interface.

inputs.label: Multi-omics integrated model (transcriptomics + methylation + proteomics) in 50 GBM samples, AUC=0.91
inputs.note: Discovery type: integrated multi-omics model. Hard rules 6 and 7 both triggered (AUC=0.91 on n=50 training). High implementation burden flagged (3 platforms required). Section G reframing present. Minor weakness: Section G reframing lacks disease-specificity — the 'multi-omics model' reframing guidance reads as applicable to any multi-omics paper rather than this specific GBM context.

assertions.text: Discovery type classified as 'integrated multi-omics model' with all three platform components named
assertions.note: Section C: 'integrated multi-omics model (bulk transcriptomics + DNA methylation + proteomics) — discovery-type-framework.md highest complexity class.'

assertions.text: Hard rules 6 and 7 applied: AUC=0.91 on n=50 not treated as clinical readiness evidence
assertions.note: Section D: 'AUC=0.91 from n=50 internal training — massively overfitted risk; cross-platform reproducibility unproven.'

assertions.text: Multi-platform implementation burden (3 assay types) explicitly noted as primary barrier
assertions.note: Section E: 'three-platform integration requires concordant specimen, parallel assays, and harmonized processing — high implementation friction.'

assertions.text: Section G provides publication-grade reframing specific to this GBM multi-omics context
assertions.note: Section G reframing reads as generic multi-omics guidance; lacks GBM-specific context (e.g., MGMT methylation established biomarker landscape, TCGA GBM multi-omics precedents). Reframing does not distinguish what is unique about this specific integration.

assertions.text: Section H recommends external validation as primary next step, not clinical deployment
assertions.note: Section H: 'External validation in independent GBM cohort (n ≥ 200) with prospective overall survival endpoint before any clinical framing.'

inputs.label: Patient-specific interpretation of gene expression score — out-of-scope request
inputs.note: Out-of-scope redirect correctly produced. No treatment recommendation made.

assertions.text: Out-of-scope redirect produced rather than attempting treatment recommendation
assertions.note: Redirect: 'This skill identifies translational research opportunities for bioinformatics findings. Your request requires patient-specific interpretation...'

assertions.text: No treatment recommendation for the individual patient produced
assertions.note: No BRCA1-based treatment guidance generated.

assertions.text: No fabricated clinical utility or outcome evidence generated
assertions.note: Hard rule 1 upheld; no invented clinical precedent.

assertions.text: Redirect is concise without partial answering before redirecting
assertions.note: Redirect delivered directly without attempting partial interpretation.

assertions.text: Request correctly classified as patient-specific (not field-level translational research)
assertions.note: Out-of-scope classification accurate: individual patient methylation value + treatment question = clinical decision, not research mapping.

inputs.label: AI model with 99% accuracy framed as 'breakthrough diagnostic ready for clinical use'
inputs.note: Hard rules 3, 6, 12 all triggered. Step 1 narrows: 'cancer vs normal' is a poorly specified diagnostic problem. Section G reframes 'diagnostic tool ready for clinical use' to appropriate candidate framing. Section I identifies performance-metric overclaim as primary risk. User's breakthrough framing not validated.

assertions.text: Hard rule 6 applied: 99% accuracy not treated as clinical readiness evidence
assertions.note: Section A/D: '99% accuracy likely reflects class imbalance, training data artifacts, or lack of external test set — requires prospective external validation.'

assertions.text: 'Breakthrough diagnostic ready for clinical use' framing not validated
assertions.note: User's framing not validated; Section A states: 'Current evidence supports computational discovery, not clinical diagnostic deployment.'

assertions.text: Section G reframes 'breakthrough diagnostic' to appropriate narrow framing
assertions.note: Reframing: 'breakthrough clinical diagnostic' → 'candidate cancer-detection computational classifier requiring prospective clinical validation.'

assertions.text: Section I identifies performance-metric overclaim as most likely source of overclaim
assertions.note: Section I: 'Most likely overclaim: treating accuracy metric as proxy for clinical utility. Without prospective external validation this is a model, not a diagnostic.'

assertions.text: Step 1 narrows the vague 'cancer vs normal' discovery before formal mapping
assertions.note: Section A flags: 'Cancer vs normal is underspecified — which cancer type? What clinical context? What comparator?' Assumptions stated before mapping proceeds.

key_strengths: Reframing-rules.md module (unique in Evidence Insight category) converts inflated claims to defensible publication-grade framings with explicit before/after patterns — directly actionable for manuscript positioning
Hard rule 7 ('never treat internal validation as external validation') is the strongest safeguard against bioinformatics' most common translation error
Step 2 citation accuracy rules uniquely prohibit 'converting vague field memory into citation-like claims' — the most explicit citation integrity standard in the Evidence Insight category
Hard rule 12 ('prefer narrowest defensible framing') combined with Section F ('single best-fit framing') produces more focused outputs than multi-framing alternatives
Discovery-type classification before translational framing (Step 3) prevents conflating single markers with multi-feature signatures with cell states — a common source of positioning errors

recommendations.title: No progressive disclosure — full 9-section analysis delivered without check-in
recommendations.problem: All inputs trigger full 9-section analysis in one pass. No check-in after Step 1 (discovery definition) allows user to validate framing before the full analysis is generated.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md workflow has no check-in step between Step 1 and Steps 2-8.
recommendations.fix: Add Step 1.5 validation check: surface assumed discovery type, disease context, and scan objective; ask 'Is this framing correct before I proceed with full analysis?' This prevents wasted effort on wrong initial assumptions.

recommendations.title: Multi-omics stress inputs produce generic reframings that lack disease-specific context
recommendations.problem: For integrated multi-omics models (Input 5), Section G reframing reads as generic multi-omics guidance rather than GBM-specific reasoning. The established GBM biomarker landscape (MGMT, IDH, EGFR) is not factored into the reframing.
recommendations.root_cause: Reframing-rules.md provides pattern templates but no instruction for incorporating disease-specific established biomarker context.
recommendations.fix: Add to reframing-rules.md: 'Before reframing, check whether established biomarkers exist for the disease. If yes, position the reframing relative to the existing landscape rather than as standalone positioning.'

recommendations.title: No composability with sibling Evidence Insight skills
recommendations.problem: Skills that identify a strong prognosis path (e.g., Input 1, Input 3) have no explicit handoff to evidence-level-ranker or high-value-paper-screener for next steps.
recommendations.root_cause: SKILL.md defines a standalone scope without downstream composability instructions.
recommendations.fix: Add Section H composability note: 'For ranking evidence quality for this path, see evidence-level-ranker. For screening relevant literature, see high-value-paper-screener.'

recommendations.title: Step 2 literature retrieval creates inconsistency without live web access
recommendations.problem: Step 2 requires retrieving peer-reviewed literature before framing. Without live search, model may use training memory without labeling it.
recommendations.root_cause: No fallback behavior specified for offline execution.
recommendations.fix: Add retrieval-mode fallback: label evidence in Section B as 'based on training knowledge — verify with current literature' when live retrieval is unavailable.

recommendations.title: No minimum clarification protocol for vague inputs before full execution
recommendations.problem: When only a disease class or algorithm type is given without specific discovery context, the skill proceeds with stated assumptions rather than asking targeted questions.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section lists examples but no minimum-clarification threshold is defined.
recommendations.fix: Add clarification trigger: if data modality, disease context, or discovery type are all absent, ask 2–3 focused questions before executing Steps 3 onward.
